title: viola tricolor subsp. hortensis
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. hortensis, commonly known as pansy or heartsease, is a flowering plant that belongs to the viola family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. hortensis include its ability to soothe and calm the mind, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a good night's sleep. The plant can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, as well as being used in cooking and as a garnish for its vibrant flowers.
